purpose: Catch vague all-of-that cost claims that summarize several actions as expensive or wasteful without naming the concrete waste.
rejectIf: sentence names the concrete cost, budget, invoice, time loss, or team affected
summary subject points to a specific preceding line item rather than a vague pile of activity
positiveExamples: All of that gets expensive when nobody knows which problem they are solving.
All this gets wasteful when no one knows what question they are answering.
negativeExamples: The migration got expensive when the API vendor raised the monthly bill.
All of that cost 14 hours because the crawler retried broken URLs.
notes: This pattern intentionally allows a when-clause because the slop frame is the vague summary subject plus abstract ignorance claim.
